look.text: Kessler, chewing, radiant with the knowledge of a man who has just eaten someone else's rye and remembered something obscure.
Kessler: hair like a startled cat, a fact for every occasion, an appetite for other people's food.

talk.speaker: target
talk.text: "1904, Artie. The answer is 1904. Also this rye is a TRIUMPH. Do you have another one?"

talk.speaker: target
talk.text: "Is that— is that the marble rye? Give it HERE, I'll tell you anything. I contain multitudes. Mostly trivia and sourdough."

talk.speaker: target
talk.text: "Trivia Night's tonight, Artie. I know EVERYTHING. I just get, y'know, peckish under pressure."

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: Kessler inhales half the loaf, gasps, and delivers the answer like a weather bulletin: "NINETEEN OH FOUR." You will never get the rye back. You didn't want it back.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "It doesn't CLOSE, Artie. That's not an umbrella, that's a commitment."

look.text: Elna: sharper than the cutlery, and keeping a running score of every wrong thing anyone has ever said within earshot.

talk.speaker: target
talk.text: "You paid a nine dollar tab with nine dollars. That's not a scheme, Artie, that's arithmetic."

talk.speaker: target
talk.text: "We WON. On a rye-powered guess from a man who thinks the Renaissance was a hotel. I've never been prouder or more annoyed."

talk.speaker: target
talk.text: "You owe Nib nine dollars. I was there. I keep score, Artie. I keep meticulous, joyless score."

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "I'm not signing anything, and neither are you. Put the pen away before it starts a fight."
